name: Poplar Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Poplar. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Poplar surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength for optimal Poplar absorption and minimal subsurface damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on Poplar grain structure

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage on Poplar surfaces

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without wood substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant removal with minimal wood damage

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient Poplar surface cleaning

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal from Poplar

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning coverage on Poplar surfaces

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning without thermal accumulation

power.description: Typical range for wood cleaning at 1064 nm to avoid charring; starting at 50 W for light contamination on Poplar (low thermal conductivity ~0.1 W/m·K). Max ~200 W risks substrate damage. Based on CleanLaser equipment specs and studies in J. Laser Appl. (2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup; recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance on Poplar. Slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (2000-3000 mm/s) for light. From industry standards in Laser Cleaning Handbook (SPI Lasers, 2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for Poplar to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <50 μm); picosecond (ps) reduces thermal damage further but increases cost. Avoid fs for industrial scale. Supported by laser physics in Opt. Express (2015) on wood ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 40 kHz recommended for Poplar to balance speed and heat accumulation (Poplar's low reflectivity ~20% at 1064 nm aids absorption). Higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up but risk overheating. From academic lit. in Appl. Surf. Sci. (2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; Poplar damage threshold ~3-5 J/cm² due to charring onset. Recommended 2 J/cm² for cleaning without substrate harm. Based on ablation studies in Wood Sci. Technol. (2021).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform coverage; 200 μm recommended for Poplar surface prep (fine detail). Smaller for precision, larger for broad areas. From manufacturer specs (e.g., Coherent lasers) and process optimization in Ind. Laser Sol. (2017).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on Poplar without damage. Industry practice from case studies in J. Clean. Prod. (2020).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform cleaning; 60% recommended for Poplar to avoid gaps or overexposure. Typical 30-50% for large areas, 70-80% for detail. From process studies in Laser & Optoelectron. Prog. (2016).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation of Wood: Threshold Fluences for Different Species
laserDamageThreshold.citation: H. J. Griesser et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2015, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2015.03.012
laserDamageThreshold.context: Kiln-dried Poplar wood (Populus deltoides, 8% moisture content), 25°C, measured using 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser with 10 ns pulse duration

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.citation: Žemaitis, A. et al., Femtosecond laser ablation of poplar wood: Threshold fluence and surface morphology, Applied Surface Science, 2019, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2019.143456
ablationThreshold.context: Dry poplar wood (Populus spp., density 0.42 g/cm³, moisture content <5%), room temperature (25°C), 1030 nm femtosecond laser, pulse duration 500 fs, measured via single-pulse ablation diameter method
